differences.title: 1. RENDERING APPROACH
differences.leftDesc: React uses a Virtual DOM for efficient updates.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS compiles JSX to direct DOM updates without a Virtual DOM.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: State changes trigger re-renders of components.
differences.rightDesc: Signals track dependencies for precise updates.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: React offers good performance with optimizations.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS is often faster due to its compilation and reactivity.

differences.title: 4. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: React's runtime adds to the bundle size.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS has a minimal runtime, resulting in smaller bundles.

differences.title: 5. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Familiar JSX syntax, but learning React concepts.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding signals and reactivity principles.

differences.title: 6. SERVER SIDE RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Robust SSR with frameworks like Next.js.
differences.rightDesc: SSR available via the SolidStart meta-framework.

differences.title: 7. ECOSYSTEM & COMMUNITY
differences.leftDesc: Huge ecosystem, many libraries and developers.
differences.rightDesc: Smaller but active and rapidly expanding community.
